title: VerificationReview
description: A verifier's verdict on a specific record (upper, Lowering, or Template), per design/verification-sketch-01.md V2 + PFS10-VR1..VR5. Carries reviewer_id, reviewed_at_τ_a, verdict (closed enum matching Python's VERDICT_* constants), anchor_τ_a (snapshot of the target's τ_a at review time), and target_record (CrossDialectRef). Optional comment and match_strength (meaningful for partial-match verdicts per V7). target_record uses inline $defs/cross_dialect_ref re-inlined from lowering.json per PFS8-V + PFS10-X1.

cross_dialect_ref.description: Typed reference to a record in a specific dialect. Re-inlined from schema/lowering/lowering.json per PFS8-V + PFS10-X1 (duplication cost bounded; lower cross-namespace coupling). Shape is identical. Open dialect-token string per PFS9-X4.

record_id.description: Stable identifier for the referenced record in its dialect.

reviewer_id.description: Stable identifier for the verifier emitting this review (e.g., 'verifier:characterization:domain-assignment'). PFS10-VR2.

reviewed_at_τ_a.description: Author-time τ_a at which the review was recorded. PFS10-VR2.

verdict.description: Closed enum matching Python's VERDICT_* constants in core/verification.py. Distinct from Lowering's AnnotationReview verdict vocabulary — the two record types have their own four-value sets (AnnotationReview emits 'rejected' where VerificationReview emits 'partial-match'). PFS10-VR3.

anchor_τ_a.description: Snapshot of the target record's τ_a at review time. If the target's current τ_a exceeds this value, downstream tooling surfaces the review as stale. PFS10-VR2.

target_record.description: The record this review attaches to, as a CrossDialectRef. PFS10-VR4.

comment.description: Optional verifier prose explaining the verdict. Python default None → omitted from dump; any set string emits unconditionally. PFS10-VR5.

match_strength.description: Optional confidence score in [0, 1]. Meaningful for partial-match verdicts per V7 (e.g., a Characterization check that holds 70% of the time). Other verdicts may set it for transparency or leave None. Python default None → omitted when None. PFS10-VR5.
